serving.description: 2 tortillas with steak and salsa (approximately 1 serving)
serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving consists of two soft flour tortillas filled with grilled steak slices and fresh salsa, weighing approximately 280 g in total.

nutrient_source.title: Beef, steak, grilled (USDA SR Legacy 13365) and Tortillas, flour, ready-to-bake or -fry (USDA SR Legacy 18364) combined with fresh salsa components; composite estimate weighted by typical recipe proportions (50% steak, 35% tortilla, 15% salsa/vegetables).
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for steak tortillas as a composite dish. Values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data: beef steak grilled (FDC ID 13365, ~50% by weight), flour tortillas (FDC ID 18364, ~35% by weight), and fresh salsa/tomato/onion/coriander (~15% by weight). Weighted composite calculation applied per 100 g of assembled dish.

gi_source.title: Tortilla, wheat flour — GI value from University of Sydney GI Database; composite GI estimated by weighting tortilla GI (52) against steak (GI ~0, no glycaemic carbohydrate) and salsa (GI ~35) components by carbohydrate contribution.
gi_source.source_match_notes: Wheat flour tortilla GI of 52 sourced from University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021). Steak contributes negligible glycaemic carbohydrate (GI effectively 0). Fresh salsa contributes minor carbohydrate with estimated GI ~35. Composite GI calculated as carbohydrate-weighted average across components, yielding approximately 52 for the assembled dish, classified as low GI. Glycaemic load per 280 g serving = (52 × 22.5 × 2.80) / 100 = 32.8 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~380 mg per 100 g; ~1064 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one tortilla and request reduced-sodium preparation, as a full two-tortilla serving provides approximately 1064 mg sodium, representing nearly half the recommended daily limit of 2300 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycaemic load (~32.8 per serving) driven primarily by refined wheat flour tortillas
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be aware that the refined flour tortillas contribute a moderate glycaemic load per serving; substituting corn or wholegrain tortillas and limiting to one tortilla per meal can help attenuate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from grilled beef steak (~2.4 g per 100 g; ~6.7 g per serving) and moderate sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should choose lean cuts of steak, limit serving to one tortilla, and pair with additional vegetables to reduce saturated fat and sodium intake within heart-healthy dietary targets.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Steak tortillas are a Mexican-origin dish consisting of grilled or pan-seared beef steak sliced and wrapped in soft wheat flour tortillas, typically accompanied by fresh salsa made from tomato, onion, coriander, and lime juice. The dish is closely related to carne asada tacos and bistec en tortilla, common street-food preparations across Mexico and widely adopted in Tex-Mex cuisine. The steak provides a savoury, slightly charred flavour with a tender, juicy texture, while the soft flour tortilla offers a mild, slightly chewy base. Fresh salsa adds brightness and acidity. Nutritionally, the dish is a moderate-energy, protein-rich meal providing approximately 218 kcal per 100 g, with 14.8 g protein, 7.2 g fat, and 22.5 g carbohydrate. The primary sodium source is the tortilla and any seasoning on the steak; a full two-tortilla serving delivers approximately 1064 mg sodium. The glycaemic index is approximately 52 (low), driven by the wheat flour tortilla, though the glycaemic load per serving is moderate at ~32.8. Clinically, the dish is relevant for individuals managing hypertension (sodium), Type 2 Diabetes (refined carbohydrate from tortilla), and cardiovascular disease (saturated fat from beef). In Singapore, this dish is served at Mexican restaurants such as Three Amigos and Fiesta Mexico, typically priced around SGD 32 per portion.
common_user_queries: How many calories are in steak tortillas?
What is the GI of steak tacos?
How much protein is in a steak wrap?
Are steak tortillas suitable for diabetics?

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for steak tortillas as a composite assembled dish. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data for grilled beef steak and flour tortillas with a weighted composite calculation. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database tortilla reference with carbohydrate-weighted composite methodology. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are high-quality approved primary sources and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source data for this composite dish. Nutrient values represent a weighted composite estimate based on approximately 50% grilled beef steak, 35% flour tortilla, and 15% fresh salsa/vegetables by weight. The dish is served at Two Singapore restaurants (Fiesta Mexico — Three Amigos Indian and Mexican Restaurant; Three Amigos Mexican and Indian — Boat Quay) at SGD 32.00 per portion. The cuisine_tag 'Naan' in the input record appears to be a data artefact from the restaurant also serving Indian cuisine and is not relevant to this food record. GI estimated using carbohydrate-weighted composite method; tortilla GI reference: Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021: a systematic review. Am J Clin Nutr. 2021;114(5):1625-1632.
